in 2026, I lost my LinkedIn following, I
have no network at all, and it was just
me, I'll show you how to use Claude in
30 days to become the top performing rep
and sales leader in your company. And
after training 50,000 reps at Google,
Salesforce, HubSpot, Snowflake, I've
seen what can work and how you can go
about it. And these four moves that I'll
walk you through and these exact prompts
is how another client generated 1.8
million dollars in pipeline from what I
am going to show you. And so, without
any further ado, let's get into it. So,
the four moves that we're going to talk
about, number one is how to make your
ICP your unfair advantage in the best
way possible. Number two is how do you
make sure your right triggers have
relevant messaging. Number three is how
do you train Claude in your voice and
not someone else's. And number four is
how do you build out outbound rhythm.
Now, let's dive into step number one,
which is the most important part, which
is your ICP. Most people, when they
build their ICP,
they don't exactly know how to do it.
And so, what that leads into random
outbound activities and a waste of your
own energy, and it's going to make you
extremely frustrated as a sales leader
and as a rep. And so, we don't also just
want to build this as a doc and forget
it. So, in 2026, as I'm recording this
video right now,
it's easier to do this with Claude than
your ICP. Your prospect really doesn't
care
that you know about their job title.
What they care about is what they do
in that job and understanding their
world. That is business acumen and is
one of the biggest luxury skills in our
environment right now. It's the one
skill that AI can't really fake. So, how
you go about this
is
going inside of Claude
and simply asking Claude, "Hey, I'm
going after VPs of marketing, VPs of the
etc."
And asking and asking Clod, what are the
top five challenges that my buyer faces?
What are the top five priorities that
they have right now? And what are they
being measured on by the board? And the
goal is you want to put this in deep
research so it can do its thing. And
Clod will give you a brief on exactly
what the buyers care about. And you can
cross analyze that with your own
product. I do this for every single
persona that I prospect for our sales
team six offer, our sales consultancy
offer. VPs of sales, CROs, head of sales
development. I know exactly what they
are looking to do, and I know what
points to talk about. I know what's
keeping them up at night, and I don't
have to ask that question because I
already know. And now my messaging is
going to hit relevant points to get more
responses. That's the unfair advantage
that a lot of people are not doing, and

just build a project. Now let's get to

point. We don't want to build random
list of outbound. We want to build
trigger list of outbound. Now, you may
be asking Warren, what's a trigger? What
are you what are you talking about? A
trigger is a compelling event
essentially. It's when a company hires a
new sales rep. A company has a product
update. They got a funding round.
They've expanded offices. It's when a
company makes a move, and based on that
move or a person maybe post something as
well that could be something too. When
they make that move, the event opens the
door for you to prospect them with
something of relevancy. For every single
person that's watching this video, from
a sales leader to an SDR, AE, whatever
you are, maybe you're a founder,
you want to identify, what are my top
five trigger points that I can use to
reach out to the buyer? So I'll give you
an example of my top five trigger points
are number one, are they hiring sales
development reps, which means that they
have an increase in pipeline coverage,
so they're hiring these people. Number
two is inside of hiring AEs in the job
responsibilities, they say that the AEs
are tasked with self-prospecting, which
means that they probably need help with
that because it ends up being an
indicator. Three is a product update.
They need go-to-market messaging to
outbound other product update to get in
front of the right people, so we know
that that's critical and that's
important. Four is they're expanding
offices or they open up a new office and
SDR hub and AE hub. I'll get to that.

coming in because they probably have an
initiative to be like, "Whoa, we need to
get more pipeline, etc." And funding can
be included in that, but you get my
point. You should at least have five of
these
that you're looking for in a company and

certain personas that you can lean on,
too, right? When they maybe join a
company or maybe uh when they you know,

a trigger point for the vertical time."

can chop it up. But let's get into the
video because we don't want to just blow
up everybody once we get this messaging
every single day. You want to focus on
the channels and how you or orchestrate
those channels. So, the main three
channels in outbound are email, call,
and LinkedIn. You can do direct mails,
you can do videos, but what I would
recommend you all to do here
is I would actually take the data that
you have from a sales engagement tool,
the data that you have from
maybe you have like a HubSpot tool that
does like an outreach as well. And then
find the times where people view your
emails and reply your emails the most,
leverage a calling tool
like most of you out there might have a
calling tool like a ConnectAndSell, a
NoExcuse, an Orum, etc. Or maybe a Gong.
And just feed it the data from there and
say, "When do people pick up the phone
the most based on the data? When do
people open and reply to emails the most
based on the data?" And then that is the
time slots that you need to take these
activities I'm telling you about and
prospect. So again, if I'm going back as
a rep, you notice what I'm doing is I'm
leveraging data that exists to find the
places where I need to spend the most
time. So, if I leverage the data and it
says, "Hey, based on all the reps across
the company, we find that at 10:00 a.m.
local time that has the most connects,
I'm cold calling at 10:00 a.m." And if
it says, "Hey, most people reply on
Fridays at 2:00 p.m., then I'm going to
send my messages Friday at 2:00 p.m. on
LinkedIn and email." This is just makes
it easier for you to condition your
behavior and condition your team's
behavior to prospect within windows that
make sense. You could be the most
skilled basketball player of all time.
Excuse the analogy I'm going with, but
be the skilled basketball player of all
time, but if you go in there and there
is a flat basketball with no air, it
doesn't matter. And so, that's what's
actually happening with a lot of reps
and teams. You're calling in in time
slots that have no activity. So, it
doesn't matter how skilled you are.
You're not even getting opportunities to
have the conversation. So, leverage your
data, map the data towards the times it
tells you to.
Your team should be calling and emailing
and LinkedIn doing those slots. And then
you can get creative now with videos and
direct mail outside of those slots,
right? And have some super fun with it,
right? And use those In my videos I talk
about it, money-making activities. You
should be going like, "Okay, from 10:00
to 11:00 I know these calls hit so, I'm
going to book meetings which is
money-making activities which is going
to help me." That's the rhythm that
compounds over time and will ultimately
make your team successful. And how you
measure all this at the end of the day
is three things. Number one is we want
to look at the reply rates that we're
getting, the positive reply rates that
are leading to meetings. Two, how many
conversations does it take you to get a
meeting? And the third is based on the
responses that we're getting, is that
actually leading into quality pipeline
and closed at the end of the day? So,
you want to look at am I getting
positive reply rates on LinkedIn at a
10%? Right? So, one out of 10 messages I
should get a reply. One out of 10 calls,
I should be getting three meetings out
of that. So, that's like how you want to
think about this as you get into the
system and leverage Claude to do that
and looking at the data. And ultimately
at the end of the day, take this
information that I'm telling you. Write
this down. Claude doesn't write your
outreach. Claude buys you back hours to
be more magnetic, intuitive, and
consistent as a human in your outreach.
That's the game. Stop asking AI to write
everything for you.
Write this email, do this cold call.
Like, don't do that.
Start asking AI to make a better version
of you based on the data that's there so
you can go out and be the person you So,
like, comment, subscribe. Happy
prospecting and we'll see you all in the
next video. Talk about some outbound
strategy.
